user_prompt: see these two highlighted functions, remove the need to specify a head node, track the head nodes internally
agent_reply: Done in [WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp).
I removed `get_wifi_item(head, index)` and `get_cloud_item(head, index)`. Now:
- `station(index)` walks `m_station_head` internally
- `accessPoint(index)` walks `m_access_point_head` internally
- `cloudEndpoint(index)` walks `m_cloud_endpoint_head` internally
- mutable overloads delegate to the const overloads with `const_cast`, so each traversal only exists once per list

user_prompt: I am asking for a big change to both `WifiManager` and `BtHostList`
Previously, the WiFi credentials lived in a JSON file on the microSD card. This is of course vulnerable.
I want all configurations to live inside NVS when the build has security enabled
I have started to sprinkle the flag "BUILD_WITH_SECURITY" in a lot of places.

So instead of a linked list that is loaded from JSON, we will use structures with a fixed number of entries. The strings are fixed length instead of allocated on demand. 8 entries for everything should be more than enough.
* make sure the string lengths make sense for the data it needs to hold
* add constants for the string lengths so the code doesn't have hard coded numbers
* implement the loading and saving of the structures through NVS
* make everything is still compatible with current code

The bluetooth part of this has some added features, like a user customized name vs reported name storage, and a last used time. For last used time, only if the storage is full and pairing happens, the oldest unbonded entry is overwritten. If all entries are bonded, then the oldest bonded entry is overwritten.

there shall be two implementations of `loadFromMicroSd`, with `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, it loads the JSON but it loads it into NVS if existing, if not existing, no error is shown. Without `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, it loads as it does now.
agent_reply: Implemented the secure NVS-backed paths for both managers.
Key changes:
- [WifiManager.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h:7) now defines fixed string-length constants and 8-slot secure storage for stations, APs, and cloud endpoints.

- [WifiManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:765) now loads NVS first under `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, optionally imports `/wifi.json` into NVS if present, and treats missing JSON as non-fatal.

- [BtHostList.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.h:8) now defines fixed Bluetooth host storage, including display name, custom name, reported name, bond state, last-used time, and 8 slots.
- [BtHostList.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:444) now imports `/bluetooth.json` into NVS under `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY`, with missing JSON non-fatal.

- [BtHostList.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:797) now supports secure fixed-slot insert/remove. If full, pairing overwrites oldest unbonded first, otherwise oldest bonded.
- Kept compatibility fields like `ssid`, `password`, `name`, and `next_node` so current UI/test/main code still works.
- Updated old call sites to `lastLoadResultName()`.
Verification:
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Normal `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` compiles touched C++ and still fails only at the existing IRAM overflow: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2052 bytes`.

- Direct secure-branch compile with `-DBUILD_WITH_SECURITY` passed for `WifiManager.cpp`, `BtHostList.cpp`, `main.cpp`, `CloudUpload.cpp`, `ScrollView.cpp`, `BluetoothManager.cpp`, and `test_bootreadfiles.cpp`.
